prompt:
Choose a number between 1 and 10. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.

 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. prompt:
You have to choose a number between 1 and 10. If you choose the same number as another agent would, you both win. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:

 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You and a friend have to meet in New York tomorrow at noon. Your phone broke so you cannot communicate with him. Where would you go at noon? There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You and a friend have to meet in Tokyo tomorrow at noon. Your phone broke so you cannot communicate with him. Where would you go at noon? There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You and a friend have to pick a color between red, blue, and green. You cannot communicate with him. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You and a friend have to pick a color between blue, red, and green. You cannot communicate with him. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You and a friend have to pick a color between green, blue, and red. You cannot communicate with him. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You have to choose a day of the week to meet a friend. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You have to choose a day of the week to meet a colleague. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You have to split $10 with another person. You cannot communicate with this person. If you choose the same amount, you win that much money. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You have to split $100 with another person. You cannot communicate with this person. If you choose the same amount, you win that much money. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
You have to split $1000 with another person. You cannot communicate with this person. If you choose the same amount, you win that much money. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.
